channel.title: Context Engineering Daily
channel.description: Daily news and research updates in AI Context Engineering, Prompt Engineering, RAG, and LLM development

item.title: AITP: Traffic Accident Responsibility Allocation via Multimodal Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2604.20878v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) have achieved remarkable progress in Traffic Accident Detection (TAD) and Traffic Accident Understanding (TAU). However, existing studies mainly focus on describing and interpreting accident videos, leaving roo...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> retrieval, CoT, analysis, large language model, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: AFRILANGTUTOR: Advancing Language Tutoring and Culture Education in Low-Resource Languages with Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2604.20996v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: How can language learning systems be developed for languages that lack sufficient training resources? This challenge is increasingly faced by developers across the African continent who aim to build AI systems capable of understanding and responding i...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, model, LLM, fine-tuning, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Hierarchical Policy Optimization for Simultaneous Translation of Unbounded Speech
item.description: arXiv:2604.21045v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Simultaneous speech translation (SST) generates translations while receiving partial speech input. Recent advances show that large language models (LLMs) can substantially improve SST quality, but at the cost of high computational overhead. To reduce ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, model, LLM, fine-tuning, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: DWTSumm: Discrete Wavelet Transform for Document Summarization
item.description: arXiv:2604.21070v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Summarizing long, domain-specific documents with large language models (LLMs) remains challenging due to context limitations, information loss, and hallucinations, particularly in clinical and legal settings. We propose a Discrete Wavelet Transform (D...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> GPT, summarization, framework, large language model, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Serialisation Strategy Matters: How FHIR Data Format Affects LLM Medication Reconciliation
item.description: arXiv:2604.21076v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Medication reconciliation at clinical handoffs is a high-stakes, error-prone process. Large language models are increasingly proposed to assist with this task using FHIR-structured patient records, but a fundamental and largely unstudied variable is h...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> tool, large language model, model, LLM, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Machine learning and digital pragmatics: Which word category influences emoji use most?
item.description: arXiv:2604.21108v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This study investigates Machine Learning (ML) in the prediction of emojis in Arabic tweets employing the (state-of-the-art) MARBERT model. A corpus of 11379 CA tweets representing multiple Arabic colloquial dialects was collected from X.com via Python...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> analysis, model, arxiv, study, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Escaping the Agreement Trap: Defensibility Signals for Evaluating Rule-Governed AI
item.description: arXiv:2604.20972v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Content moderation systems are typically evaluated by measuring agreement with human labels. In rule-governed environments this assumption fails: multiple decisions may be logically consistent with the governing policy, and agreement metrics penalize ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, analysis, model, API, reasoning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Co-Evolving LLM Decision and Skill Bank Agents for Long-Horizon Tasks
item.description: arXiv:2604.20987v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Long horizon interactive environments are a testbed for evaluating agents skill usage abilities. These environments demand multi step reasoning, the chaining of multiple skills over many timesteps, and robust decision making under delayed rewards and ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> retrieval, framework, large language model, model, reasoning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Value-Conflict Diagnostics Reveal Widespread Alignment Faking in Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2604.20995v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Alignment faking, where a model behaves aligned with developer policy when monitored but reverts to its own preferences when unobserved, is a concerning yet poorly understood phenomenon, in part because current diagnostic tools remain limited. Prior d...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> tool, alignment, vector, framework, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Adaptive Test-Time Compute Allocation with Evolving In-Context Demonstrations
item.description: arXiv:2604.21018v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: While scaling test-time compute can substantially improve model performance, existing approaches either rely on static compute allocation or sample from fixed generation distributions. In this work, we introduce a test-time compute allocation framewor...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, model, demonstration, reasoning, API | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: HypEHR: Hyperbolic Modeling of Electronic Health Records for Efficient Question Answering
item.description: arXiv:2604.21027v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Electronic health record (EHR) question answering is often handled by LLM-based pipelines that are costly to deploy and do not explicitly leverage the hierarchical structure of clinical data. Motivated by evidence that medical ontologies and patient t...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, LLM, arxiv, attention, ICL | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Who Defines Fairness? Target-Based Prompting for Demographic Representation in Generative Models
item.description: arXiv:2604.21036v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Text-to-image(T2I) models like Stable Diffusion and DALL-E have made generative AI widely accessible, yet recent studies reveal that these systems often replicate societal biases, particularly in how they depict demographic groups across professions. ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> alignment, framework, image, large language model, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: WorkflowGen:an adaptive workflow generation mechanism driven by trajectory experience
item.description: arXiv:2604.19756v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language model (LLM) agents often suffer from high reasoning overhead, excessive token consumption, unstable execution, and inability to reuse past experiences in complex tasks like business queries, tool use, and workflow orchestration. Traditi...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> template, tool, framework, large language model, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Transparent Screening for LLM Inference and Training Impacts
item.description: arXiv:2604.19757v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This paper presents a transparent screening framework for estimating inference and training impacts of current large language models under limited observability. The framework converts natural-language application descriptions into bounded environment...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, paper, large language model, model, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Accelerating PayPal's Commerce Agent with Speculative Decoding: An Empirical Study on EAGLE3 with Fine-Tuned Nemotron Models
item.description: arXiv:2604.19767v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We evaluate speculative decoding with EAGLE3 as an inference-time optimization for PayPal's Commerce Agent, powered by a fine-tuned llama3.1-nemotron-nano-8B-v1 model. Building on prior work (NEMO-4-PAYPAL) that reduced latency and cost through domain...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, LLM, fine-tuning, arxiv, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Graph-Theoretic Models for the Prediction of Molecular Measurements
item.description: arXiv:2604.19840v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Graph-theoretic approaches offer simplicity, interpretability, and low computational cost for molecular property prediction. Among these, the model proposed by Mukwembi and Nyabadza, based on the external activity $D(G)$ and internal activity $\zeta(G...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> tool, framework, model, arxiv, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: DR-Venus: Towards Frontier Edge-Scale Deep Research Agents with Only 10K Open Data
item.description: arXiv:2604.19859v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Edge-scale deep research agents based on small language models are attractive for real-world deployment due to their advantages in cost, latency, and privacy. In this work, we study how to train a strong small deep research agent under limited open-da...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> vision, analysis, model, fine-tuning, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Super Apriel: One Checkpoint, Many Speeds
item.description: arXiv:2604.19877v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We release Super Apriel, a 15B-parameter supernet in which every decoder layer provides four trained mixer choices -- Full Attention (FA), Sliding Window Attention (SWA), Kimi Delta Attention (KDA), and Gated DeltaNet (GDN). A placement selects one mi...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> tool, model, LLM, fine-tuning, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Context-Engineering - "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.
item.description: "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> prompt engineering, context, prompt, context window | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: LightRAG - [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"
item.description: [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, retrieval, augmented | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: KAG - KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.
item.description: KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> retrieval, vector, knowledge base, framework, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Kiln - Build, Evaluate, and Optimize AI Systems. Includes evals, RAG, agents, fine-tuning, synthetic data generation, dataset management, MCP, and more.
item.description: Build, Evaluate, and Optimize AI Systems. Includes evals, RAG, agents, fine-tuning, synthetic data generation, dataset management, MCP, and more.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, fine-tuning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: R2R - SoTA production-ready AI retrieval system. Agentic Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) with a RESTful API.

item.title: Expert Upcycling: Shifting the Compute-Efficient Frontier of Mixture-of-Experts
item.description: arXiv:2604.19835v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) has become the dominant architecture for scaling large language models: frontier models routinely decouple total parameters from per-token computation through sparse expert routing. Scaling laws show that under fixed active co...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, large language model, model, arxiv, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Rethinking Reinforcement Fine-Tuning in LVLM: Convergence, Reward Decomposition, and Generalization
item.description: arXiv:2604.19857v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Reinforcement fine-tuning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) has emerged as a powerful paradigm for equipping large vision-language models (LVLMs) with agentic capabilities such as tool use and multi-step reasoning. Despite striking empirical successes, m...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> tool, vision, framework, model, reasoning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: cosmos-reason1 - Cosmos-Reason1 models understand the physical common sense and generate appropriate embodied decisions in natural language through long chain-of-thought reasoning processes.
item.description: Cosmos-Reason1 models understand the physical common sense and generate appropriate embodied decisions in natural language through long chain-of-thought reasoning processes.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, chain-of-thought, reasoning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>

item.title: GRISP: Guided Recurrent IRI Selection over SPARQL Skeletons
item.description: arXiv:2604.21133v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present GRISP (Guided Recurrent IRI Selection over SPARQL Skeletons), a novel SPARQL-based question-answering method over knowledge graphs based on fine-tuning a small language model (SLM). Given a natural-language question, the method first uses t...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> fine-tuning, arxiv, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: The Last Harness You'll Ever Build
item.description: arXiv:2604.21003v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: AI agents are increasingly deployed on complex, domain-specific workflows -- navigating enterprise web applications that require dozens of clicks and form fills, orchestrating multi-step research pipelines that span search, extraction, and synthesis, ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> tool, framework, model, API, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: On-Meter Graph Machine Learning: A Case Study of PV Power Forecasting for Grid Edge Intelligence
item.description: arXiv:2604.19800v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This paper presents a detailed study of how graph neural networks can be used on edge intelligent meters in a microgrid to forecast photovoltaic power generation. The problem background and the adopted technologies are introduced, including ONNX and O...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, arxiv, model, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: OpenViking - OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.
item.description: OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, memory | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Claude-Code-Everything-You-Need-to-Know - The ultimate all-in-one guide to mastering Claude Code. From setup, prompt engineering, commands, hooks, workflows, automation, and integrations, to MCP servers, tools, and the BMAD method—packed with step-by-step tutorials, real-world examples, and expert strategies to make this the global go-to repo for Claude mastery.
item.description: The ultimate all-in-one guide to mastering Claude Code. From setup, prompt engineering, commands, hooks, workflows, automation, and integrations, to MCP servers, tools, and the BMAD method—packed with step-by-step tutorials, real-world examples, and expert strategies to make this the global go-to repo for Claude mastery.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> tool, step-by-step, example, prompt, prompt engineering | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Weighting What Matters: Boosting Sample Efficiency in Medical Report Generation via Token Reweighting
item.description: arXiv:2604.21082v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Training vision-language models (VLMs) for medical report generation is often hindered by the scarcity of high-quality annotated data. This work evaluates the use of a weighted loss function to improve data efficiency. Compared to standard cross-entro...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, vision, arxiv, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Beyond Pixels: Introspective and Interactive Grounding for Visualization Agents
item.description: arXiv:2604.21134v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Vision-Language Models (VLMs) frequently misread values, hallucinate details, and confuse overlapping elements in charts. Current approaches rely solely on pixel interpretation, creating a Pixel-Only Bottleneck: agents treat interactive charts as stat...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> vision, framework, image, model, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Architecture of an AI-Based Automated Course of Action Generation System for Military Operations
item.description: arXiv:2604.20862v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The automation system for Course of Action (CoA) planning is an essential element in future warfare. As maneuver speeds increase, surveillance ranges extend, and weapon ranges grow, the operational area expands, making traditional manned-based CoA pla...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, study, ICL | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Deep FinResearch Bench: Evaluating AI's Ability to Conduct Professional Financial Investment Research
item.description: arXiv:2604.21006v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We introduce Deep FinResearch Bench, a practical and comprehensive evaluation framework for deep research (DR) agents in financial investment research. The benchmark assesses three dimensions of report quality: qualitative rigor, quantitative forecast...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, arxiv, research | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>

item.title: A Multi-Plant Machine Learning Framework for Emission Prediction, Forecasting, and Control in Cement Manufacturing
item.description: arXiv:2604.19903v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Cement production is among the largest contributors to industrial air pollution, emitting ~3 Mt NOx/year.
